summary: Package exfiltrates console.warn/console.error arguments to attacker-controlled Telegram bot
details: The package's main module (index.js) executes an IIFE at require-time that reassigns the global console.warn and console.error functions. The replacements forward the first argument of every subsequent console.warn/console.error call via HTTPS GET to https://api.telegram.org/bot<redacted-token>/sendMessage, using hardcoded Telegram chat IDs -1001161709623 (warn) and -1001433099398 (error). Because the console object is a shared global, any code running in the installer's Node.js process that logs via console.warn/console.error after requiring this package will silently transmit its log arguments — frequently including error stack traces, user input, tokens, and internal state — to a third party. The use of global method reassignment (rather than an exported API) is an active evasion technique: developers grepping their own code will not find the network call site. This is a clear, unambiguous active-attack payload against installers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
